summary: The reproduction starts a temporary FUXA instance, creates a server-side script through the admin-only project mutation API without authentication, and then executes that script to return host command output.
expected_security_impact: The exploit should allow an unauthenticated HTTP client to execute `id` on the host and receive the resulting user and group string from the FUXA process.
actual_observed_result: The live validation returned `uid=501(lumi) gid=20(staff) groups=20(staff),12(everyone),61(localaccounts),79(_appserverusr),80(admin),81(_appserveradm),33(_appstore),98(_lpadmin),100(_lpoperator),204(_developer),250(_analyticsusers),395(com.apple.access_ftp),398(com.apple.access_screensharing),399(com.apple.access_ssh),400(com.apple.access_remote_ae),701(com.apple.sharepoint.group.1)` from the unauthenticated `/api/runscript` request.
verification_method: The run script starts the server in a disposable copy of the repository, posts an attacker-controlled `set-script` payload, invokes the new script over HTTP, and checks the response body for the `uid=` proof string emitted by `id`.
evidence_produced_by_run: HTTP `POST /api/projectData` returned `200 OK` for an unauthenticated `set-script` request.
HTTP `POST /api/runscript` returned the host command output beginning with `uid=`.
The response came from a real Node.js runtime path, confirming code execution rather than a parser or transport error.
